data.narrative:
Kepler Communications is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with particular focus on 3 same-stage private comparables in the sector: Sierra Space ($8.0B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Skyryse ($1.1B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $340M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies. A public-sector P/S median of 25.8x is available across the 6 listed tickers but lacks applicability here.

The triangulated valuation range is $289M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $920M. The wide dispersion reflects material variance in peer outcomes—particularly Sierra Space's outlier valuation—and the inherent uncertainty in stage-progression multiples for early-stage deep-tech ventures. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the Sierra Space comp and whether $200M raised adequately proxies current run-rate performance relative to peers.
